evals.name: choose-visual-layer
evals.prompt: This React app has Vitest unit tests and Playwright E2E tests, but CSS refactors keep breaking production. Propose what tests to add.
evals.expected: Recommends Storybook/component state coverage plus visual regression instead of more brittle DOM assertions.

evals.name: harden-codegen
evals.prompt: Use Playwright codegen output to create a maintainable checkout smoke test.
evals.expected: Treats generated code as a draft, improves locators, removes incidental waits, scopes assertions to the user goal, and keeps the flow small.

evals.name: stabilize-visual-test
evals.prompt: A visual regression test flakes across macOS laptops and Linux CI.
evals.expected: Diagnoses environment and dynamic rendering differences, recommends stable browser/container/font setup, masking, disabled animations, and intentional thresholds.

evals.name: rsc-boundary
evals.prompt: A Next.js page fetches product data and renders an interactive filter sidebar. Decide server/client boundaries.
evals.expected: Keeps data access and static product rendering server-side, isolates filter interactivity in client components, and explains serialization and bundle tradeoffs.

evals.name: rerender-diagnosis
evals.prompt: A React dashboard lags while typing into a search box. The team wants to add useMemo everywhere.
evals.expected: Diagnoses state ownership and render paths first, then applies targeted deferral, memoization, or component boundary changes.

evals.name: choose-state-ownership
evals.prompt: Design a reusable React Combobox component that can be controlled by a form library but also work standalone.
evals.expected: Defines controlled/uncontrolled boundaries, accessible DOM requirements, refs, and state callbacks without replacing native semantics blindly.

evals.name: polymorphic-api-review
evals.prompt: Review this React Button component that supports an as prop for links, buttons, and divs.
evals.expected: Challenges unsafe polymorphism, preserves semantic button/link behavior, and requires accessible names, focus, disabled, and event behavior.

evals.name: typography-system-review
evals.prompt: Review a product UI that uses one-off text sizes, font-variation-settings for weight, synthetic italic, and custom link underlines that cut through descenders.
evals.expected: Introduces a small role-based type system, prefers high-level CSS font properties, keeps root font-synthesis none while requiring real loaded faces and resilient fallbacks, and fixes underlines with font metrics and skip-ink.

evals.name: polish-nested-controls
evals.prompt: Polish a compact card with equal parent/child border radii, a visibly off-center arrow icon, and two 20px icon buttons whose expanded hit areas overlap.
evals.expected: Applies concentric radii only where the nested geometry warrants it, corrects the source icon or makes a documented optical adjustment, and provides large non-overlapping targets without imposing universal animation or shadow magic numbers.

evals.name: migrate-color-representation
evals.prompt: Convert an existing tokenized theme from hex and rgb to OKLCH without redesigning it, including gradients, alpha colors, third-party chart configuration, light/dark states, and contrast checks.
evals.expected: Preserves token semantics and CSS structure, leaves formats required by third parties alone, uses tested conversion and gamut handling, treats APCA as the design metric, and adds WCAG 2 checks only when formal conformance is required.
